summary: Backend testing complete for InScale Digital Booking API. 29/29 pytest tests passed covering health, services catalog, availability rules, public bookings (create + all validation/conflict cases), JWT cookie auth (login/me/logout/brute-force lockout), and admin endpoints (list/update/delete/stats with auth gating). All endpoints behave as specified.

minor.issue: Endpoint requires auth (Depends(get_current_user)); calling logout without a valid session returns 401. Typically logout should be idempotent (return 200 even if not authenticated). Low priority.

Login returns access_token in JSON body in addition to setting httpOnly cookies. If cookie auth is the source of truth, exposing the token in the body weakens the httpOnly protection. Consider removing it from the body.
secure=False on auth cookies. Acceptable for local/dev but ensure production deployment flips this to True (samesite=none + secure=true) for cross-site cookie auth.

action_items: Optional: stop returning access_token in login response body (cookies already set).
When deploying to prod, flip cookie secure=True and configure FRONTEND_URL/CORS accordingly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 562-line file; acceptable for current scope but should be split into routers (auth, bookings, admin, services) if app grows.
Brute force key uses ip:email — behind reverse proxies request.client.host may be the proxy IP; consider trusting X-Forwarded-For only if proxy is configured.
Bcrypt hash format verified at runtime (admin seed produces $2b$ hashes). Seed_admin correctly updates password if env changes (idempotent).
MongoDB _id is correctly excluded via projection on every read; no _id leaks in responses.
All endpoints validated with Pydantic; status codes (200/400/401/403/404/409/429) are appropriate.
CORS configured with explicit origins + emergent regex + allow_credentials=True — correct for cookie auth.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed bookings during runs and clean them up via admin DELETE in the last test class. login_attempts collection is also cleared in a session-scope teardown to avoid leftover lockouts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_inscale_api.py -v. The brute-force test uses a unique random email per run so it does NOT lock out the real admin. A session teardown clears db.login_attempts. All 29 cases passed; admin credentials valid; bcrypt hash $2b$; httpOnly cookies confirmed via Set-Cookie header inspection.
